ambiguities.aspect: accounting treatment of R&D raw materials
options: Capitalize as R&D inventory asset (if materials can be stored and used over time)
Expense immediately as R&D expense (if materials consumed immediately or policy requires expensing)
ambiguities.clarification_question: Were these raw materials purchased for immediate use in R&D activities, or will they be held in inventory for ongoing R&D projects?
ambiguities.why_entry_differs: Immediate consumption debits R&D Expense directly; materials held for future use debit R&D Materials Inventory (asset), with expense recognized upon consumption
ambiguities.why_not_resolved: Transaction states 'purchased for R&D' but does not indicate timing of use. Accounting standards allow both treatments depending on consumption pattern: IAS 38 requires R&D expensing when incurred, but materials not yet consumed remain inventory. No convention defaults this.
